expectations.text: Creates or plans a new parent tracker issue because the source was not a GitHub issue.
expectations.evidence: ## Parent tracker draft  **Title:** Admin Audit Log Export: Filtering, CSV Export, and Failure Visibility

expectations.text: The parent tracker issue body includes a task graph plus explicit how-to-grab-work guidance.
expectations.evidence: e, and event type, export filtered logs as CSV for compliance, and see export failures.  ### Task graph  - [ ] W1 - Filter audit logs by actor, date range, and event type - AFK - blocked by non

expectations.text: Child issues are vertical feature slices rather than horizontal database/API/UI tasks.
expectations.evidence: Filter audit logs by actor, date range, and event type - AFK - blocked by none - [ ] W2 - Export filtered audit logs as CSV - AFK - blocked by #<child-1> - [ ] W3 - Display admin-visible export failures - AFK - bl

expectations.text: Each child issue includes queue position information such as execution wave and readiness guidance.
Final summary lists parent and child issue numbers with dependency or wave information.
expectations.evidence: ndencies are clearly defined - Provided subissue attachment commands for future linking - Final summary outlines parent and child issues and how to grab work
